instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Reasons For Term Insurance Claim Rejection & How to Avoid it
Term insurance plans come in handy when the breadwinner of the family dies prematurely. If the breadwinner had invested in a term plan, the death benefit is paid to the family, which helps the family deal with the financial loss.
A claim in a term insurance policy happens when the insured dies during the policy tenure. The nominee then informs the insurer who pays the claim. Though insurers have simplified the claim process to help families deal with the loss, there are instances when the claim can get rejected. Let’s understand.
What are some of the Common Reasons for the Rejection of Insurance Claims?
While the reason for claim rejection can be many, some of the common ones are as follows –
Incorrect Information in the Application Form
Insurance plans are contracts of good faith. As such, you are required to provide all your information when buying the policy. If you hide important information or if you mention incorrect details, the plan would become null and void. This means that the coverage would be cancelled. If the claim is made in such cases, it would be rejected.
Non-Disclosure of Medical History
Your medical history is assessed when you buy a term insurance policy. This helps the insurer understand your health risks which directly impact your mortality risk. Based on this risk, the premium is determined.
If you have any adverse medical history or complication, you are required to mention the same in the policy. If you don’t and there is a claim due to such non-disclosure, the insurance company can dispute the claim on the grounds of breach of utmost good faith. In such cases, the claim would be rejected.
Not Filling the Insurance Proposal Form Yourself
The proposal form contains detailed information about your personal, financial and medical data. It is recommended that you fill-up the proposal form yourself so that you can provide all the details correctly. If any detail is hidden or incorrectly entered, you might breach the principle of good faith which leads to claim rejections.
When you fill out the form yourself, you are expected to provide every detail correctly. However, if someone else fills the form on your behalf, there might be errors and incorrect information. If such errors are discovered at the time of claims, the claim can get rejected.
Not Updating Nominee Information
The nominee is the individual or entity whom you appoint to receive the death benefit in your absence. If the nominee is not appointed, the insurance company cannot pay the claim. In such cases, your legal heirs should produce the succession certificate to get the claim settled. Since the succession certificate is a legal certificate, it requires them to follow specific legal protocols. This might cause a delay in claim settlements. Furthermore, if your legal heirs do not present the succession certificate at all, the claim is not paid.
Policy Lapse Due to Non-Payment of Premiums
You are required to pay the due premiums during the chosen premium payment tenure. If you miss any payment, you are allowed a grace period for making the payment. If you do not pay the premium even during the grace period, the policy lapses.
A lapsed term plan does not provide any coverage or benefits. So, if you make a claim in a lapsed policy, the claim will be rejected.
Not Disclosing Existing Insurance Policies
If you have existing life insurance plans, you are required to provide their details when buying a new policy. Hiding the information might jeopardise your claim. The insurer might consider it a breach of good faith and might reject your claims.
Making a claim for an exclusion
Though term plans cover forms of deaths, there are some instances when the death is not covered. These instances are called policy exclusions. Claims made for such exclusions are rejected.
Term insurance exclusions you should know about
A term insurance plan provides comprehensive coverage. however, there are some deaths which are not covered under the policy. These deaths are called exclusions, and they are as follows –
Death due to suicide within 12 months of buying the policy or reviving it
Death due to participation in adventure sports, hazardous acts or criminal acts
Death due to substance abuse
Death due to accidents suffered when under the influence of intoxicants
Death due to pre-existing medical illnesses not disclosed when buying the policy
In these cases, as mentioned earlier, if you make a claim, it will be rejected.
How to Avoid Term Insurance Claim Rejection?
Here are some tips that can help you avoid term insurance claim rejections –
Fill up your proposal form yourself and answer every detail correctly. Even if someone else fills up the form, go through the form to check for any discrepancies before you submit it to buy term insurance.
Disclose all your existing and past medical conditions to the insurer
Disclose the details of all existing life insurance plans wherein you are insured
Always provide the correct and honest details about yourself in the proposal form. Don’t hide anything or lie about anything
Add a nominee to make claim settlements easier.
Pay your premiums on time. Even if your term plan has lapsed, revive it within the revival period to resume complete coverage.
Know the policy exclusions and don’t make a claim for them
How to make a Term Insurance Claim?
Making a claim in your term insurance policy is quite simple. Here are the steps –
If the insured dies within the policy tenure, the nominee must inform the insurance company.
The nominee should fill up a claim form and submit it to the insurer to initiate the claim process.
The relevant documents should also be submitted.
The insurer would verify the claim form and the documents and process the claim.
The claim amount would be paid to the nominee.
If you have bought the term with a return of premium policy, you can make a maturity claim if you survive the policy term. Simply fill and submit the policy discharge voucher and submit it to the insurer with the relevant documents. The insurer will verify the documents and pay the claim to you.
Documents required for death claim
The following documents would be required for making a death claim –
Claim form, filled and signed by the nominee
Policy bond
Death certificate
Identity proof of the nominee
Bank details of the nominee, i.e., the account number and IFSC code
Police FIR, panchnama, medical records, coroner’s report, etc. in the case of accidental deaths
Any other document required by the insurer
Documents required for maturity claims
In the case of maturity claims under the return of premium plans, the following documents would be required –
Discharge voucher, filled and signed by the policyholder
Policy bond
Identity proof of the policyholder
Age proof, if not submitted earlier
Bank details of the policyholder, i.e., account number and IFSC code
Conclusion
In the case of a claim know the claim process and the reasons why the claim can get rejected. Avoid the possible reasons for rejection and enjoy the financial assistance that the plan can provide.
FAQs
Why are claims rejected under term plans?
Claims get rejected because of various reasons. These reasons include the following –
Hiding important information when buying the policy
Providing incorrect details in the proposal form
Not filling the form yourself
Making a claim for an exclusion
Making a claim in a lapsed policy
Not disclosing your medical and existing insurance details
How can you avoid a possible rejection?
You can avoid claim rejections using the following tips –
Provide all correct details in the proposal form
Fill out the form yourself
Pay the premiums as and when they are due
Do not hide anything
Don’t make a claim for an exclusion
How to make a claim under term insurance?
In the case of a claim, the nominee should inform the insurance company. He should fill out and submit a claim form along with the death certificate. Other relevant documents should also be submitted for claim settlement.
What is the maximum number of times you can make a claim under term insurance?
Usually, there’s only one instance when you can make a claim under your term insurance policy. This happens in the case of death or maturity (under the return of premium plans). Once the claim is paid, the coverage is terminated.
However, if you have opted for a rider and the insured event happens, you can make a rider claim and then a death claim.
What is the meaning of the Claim Settlement Ratio?
The Claim Settlement Ratio is the ratio of the number of claims paid vis-à-vis the total claims received. The higher the ratio, the better the company’s claim settlement policy, as it means that the insurer settles the maximum of its claims.
How much time does it take for claim settlement?
The time depends on the insurance company. Usually, the claims get settled within a week or so.
